title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The USS Constitution led over 60 international tall ships and military vessels into Boston Harbor for Sail Boston 250.
The event featured ships from more than 20 countries, docking at various Boston waterfront locations for their final tour stop.
Thousands of visitors attended the six-day event celebrating Boston's maritime history, highlighted by a Parade of Sail.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox drafted Jake Schaffner from UNC as the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, slashed .356/.467/.552 with 6 home runs and 50 RBI.
Schaffner excelled defensively with 3 errors in 68 games, but may shift to second base due to arm strength.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston Grand Parade of Sail featured historic ships, including the USS Constitution and smaller replica schooners.
Spectators lined up from Castle Island to Piers Park to witness the rare gathering of historic vessels.
The event highlighted Boston's maritime history, attracting visitors and involving volunteers and young students in the festivities.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts, earning him the 'Cy Yuk' award from The Athletic for poor performance.
Bello excelled in relief with a 0.71 ERA across 25.1 innings, but was sent to Triple-A despite strong bullpen stats.
The Red Sox opted to call up Eduardo Rivera over Bello for a starting role in place of injured Ranger Suarez.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox are on a seven-game winning streak, close to an American League wild-card spot.
Boston faces decision to buy or sell at the trade deadline, reflecting past indecision issues.
GM Craig Breslow's job could hinge on team's decision and performance in upcoming months.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden on July 10, 2026, as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
Her performance included a mix of old favorites like 'Hips Don't Lie' and new tracks from her Grammy-winning album.
Shakira's show featured diverse dance styles, elaborate stage sets, and highlighted themes of resilience and empowerment.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Governor Maura Healey announced $18.6 million in tax credits for 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects involve converting historic properties, like mills, into housing in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
The Housing Development Incentive Program aims to address high housing costs and revitalize economically strategic communities.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, from Townsend, MA, was arrested by FBI SWAT in Albany, NY, connected to two armed bank robberies.
Sawyer allegedly robbed banks in Nashua, NH, and Boonton, NJ, stealing $6,000 and $12,000, with a semiautomatic handgun.
The arrest was part of Operation Summer Heat 2.0, involving multiple FBI field offices and local police departments.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead near his motorcycle off Route 2 in Shelburne, NH, missing since July 4.
Berlin police sought assistance from NH State Police; a resident found Grondin's body and motorcycle Thursday night.
Preliminary investigation suggests Grondin crossed into oncoming lane, struck a post; no other vehicles involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Governor Maura Healey declares July 11 "Noah Kahan Day" in Massachusetts, honoring the singer's contributions and mental health advocacy.
Noah Kahan makes history with four sold-out concerts at Fenway Park, attracting over 150,000 fans in total.
The Vermont-born artist, who lived in Watertown, is praised for songs that capture New England's essence and experiences.
